name: Fritto Misto
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sifted flour, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
2. Beat the egg yolks until light and creamy.
3. Add the beer to the dry ingredients and mix until well-blended.
4. Stir in the melted butter. Allow the batter to stand at room temperature for 1 1/2 hours.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form.
6. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
7. Heat oil for deep frying to 375°F (190°C).
8. Dip pieces of vegetables, fish, seafood, cheese, and other desired ingredients into the batter.
9. Fry each item in batches until golden brown for 2 or 3 minutes.
Variations:
- Almost any vegetable, fish, or seafood can be used in various forms for a mixed fry.
- Onions: Cut into 1/4-inch rings and coat with flour before dipping into the batter.
- Eggplant: Slice without peeling into half moons or rectangular fingers about 1/2 inch thick. Coat with flour before dipping into batter.
- Cauliflower: Break into florets.
- Broccoli: Peel stems and cut vegetables into bite-size florets.
- Tomatoes: Do not peel. Cut into 1/2-inch slices, discard seeds, and coat with flour before dipping into batter.
- Cheese: Use Gruyere, Swiss, or fontina cheese cut into 1-inch squares and 1/2 inch thick.
- Shrimp: Shell and devein raw shrimp, leaving the last segment of the tail intact. Wash and dry with paper towels. Dip in flour before dipping into batter.
- Crab meat: Select large pieces of lump crabmeat. Dust with flour before dipping into batter.
- Oysters: Use whole. Dust with flour before dipping into batter.
- Lobster or rock lobster tail: Cut meat into 1/2-inch medallions. Dust with flour before dipping into batter.
- Fish fillets: Cut into strips about 1 inch wide. Dust with flour before dipping into batter.
